info.title: Octolane Public API
info.description: OpenAPI specification for Octolane public APIs. These endpoints let external systems read CRM data and create or update accounts, contacts, opportunities, notes, and activities through the public API gateway.

tags.name: CRM
tags.description: Public endpoints for creating, bulk creating, listing, searching, and updating CRM data.

get.description: Returns accounts using cursor pagination with optional search and field projection. Custom attribute names and value types are returned in `data.custom_field_definitions`; keys in that map match keys under each account's `custom_fields` object.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque pagination cursor returned by a previous page.

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: Optional fields to include. Accepts a comma-separated string or repeated array values.

401.description: The API key is missing, invalid, or not allowed to access this route.

429.description: The route rate limit was exceeded.

post.description: Creates an account from a domain, or returns the existing account when the domain is already present. Additional account fields are persisted as system or custom attributes when supported.

requestBody.description: Account payload.

401.description: The API key is missing, invalid, or not allowed to access this route.

429.description: The route rate limit was exceeded.

post.description: Creates or resolves up to 100 account records in one request.

requestBody.description: Array of account payloads. Maximum 100 items.

401.description: The API key is missing, invalid, or not allowed to access this route.

429.description: The route rate limit was exceeded.

post.description: Search accounts with structured filters, sort, cursor pagination, and field projection. Custom attribute names and value types are returned in `data.custom_field_definitions`; keys in that map match keys under each account's `custom_fields` object.

requestBody.description: Search body with optional text query, structured filters, sort, cursor pagination, and field projection.

401.description: The API key is missing, invalid, or not allowed to access this route.

429.description: The route rate limit was exceeded.

get.description: Returns opportunities in a required pipeline using cursor pagination with optional stage, search, and field projection. Custom attribute names and value types are returned in `data.custom_field_definitions`; keys in that map match keys under each opportunity's `custom_fields` object.

parameters.name: pipeline_id
parameters.description: Pipeline ID. Required for opportunity reads.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque pagination cursor returned by a previous page.

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: Optional fields to include. Accepts a comma-separated string or repeated array values.

401.description: The API key is missing, invalid, or not allowed to access this route.

429.description: The route rate limit was exceeded.

post.description: Creates an opportunity in a pipeline and resolves related account, primary contact, additional contacts, owner, and stage from the supplied identifiers.

requestBody.description: Opportunity payload.

401.description: The API key is missing, invalid, or not allowed to access this route.

post.description: Search opportunities in a required pipeline with optional stage, structured filters, sort, cursor pagination, and field projection. Custom attribute names and value types are returned in `data.custom_field_definitions`; keys in that map match keys under each opportunity's `custom_fields` object.

requestBody.description: Opportunity search body. `pipeline_id` is required.

post.description: Creates up to 100 opportunities with nested account and contact payloads. This optimized endpoint batch-resolves accounts, contacts, owners, and stages before inserting opportunities.

requestBody.description: Array of fast bulk opportunity payloads. Maximum 100 items.

200.description: Opportunities were created or skipped as duplicates.

post.description: Updates opportunity field definitions for a pipeline in bulk. Identify each field by `id`/`attribute_id` or `property_name`. Opportunity fields are pipeline-scoped, so use this pipeline endpoint. `property_name` is a stable identifier and cannot be renamed; if you include it with `id`/`attribute_id`, it must match the stored field `property_name` exactly.

requestBody.description: Array of field definition patches. Maximum 100 items.

delete.description: Soft deletes a stage in a pipeline. If deals are in that stage, they are reassigned to `destination_stage_id` when supplied, otherwise to the best remaining stage in the pipeline.

parameters.name: destination_stage_id
parameters.description: Optional stage ID to receive deals from the deleted stage.

401.description: The API key is missing, invalid, or not allowed to access this route.

429.description: The route rate limit was exceeded.

patch.description: Updates up to 50 existing objects. The request body is keyed by object UUID, and each value is a patch object of field names to updated values. For opportunity relationship fields, `opportunity_account`, `opportunity_contact`, and `opportunity_other_stakeholders` use replace semantics: supplying the field clears the existing mapping of that type, then writes the supplied UUID or UUID list. Use `null` to clear `opportunity_account` or `opportunity_contact`; use `null` or `[]` to clear `opportunity_other_stakeholders`.

requestBody.description: Object keyed by target object UUID. Maximum 50 keys.

401.description: The API key is missing, invalid, or not allowed to access this route.

429.description: The route rate limit was exceeded.

ApiKeyAuth.name: x-api-key
ApiKeyAuth.description: Public API key for the Octolane organization.

FieldDefinition.description: Field or attribute definition. Account/contact fields use `id`; pipeline fields use `attribute_id`. Use `property_name` as the stable API key.

property_name.description: Optional stable field key. If omitted, Octolane generates a unique property name.

metadata.description: Optional field metadata. For select fields, include `select_options` with label/value/color/order entries.

FieldUpdateInput.description: Field patch. Identify account/contact fields with `id` or `property_name`; identify pipeline fields with `attribute_id`/`id` or `property_name`. `property_name` is immutable: use the same value returned by the field list/create APIs. When sent together with `id` or `attribute_id`, it must match the stored field `property_name`.

attribute_id.description: Alias for `id` when updating pipeline fields.

property_name.description: Stable field identifier. This value cannot be changed by update APIs. If supplied with `id` or `attribute_id`, it must match the stored field `property_name` exactly.

attribute_type.description: Can be changed only for custom fields, not system fields.

RecordCustomFields.description: Custom field values keyed by custom field slug. Use the sibling `custom_field_definitions` map in the same response to get each custom field's display name (`current_name`), stable property name (`property_name`), and value type (`attribute_type`).

CustomFieldDefinition.description: Definition for one custom field returned under a record's `custom_fields` object.

current_name.description: Display name configured for the custom field.

property_name.description: Stable internal property name for the custom field. This can be used in field selection, filtering, and update payloads where custom properties are accepted.

attribute_type.description: Value type for this custom field. Use this to format request values and interpret `custom_fields` values.

metadata.description: Optional field metadata such as select options. Shape depends on `attribute_type`.

CustomFieldDefinitions.description: Map of custom field slug to definition. This is returned by `/v1/accounts`, `/v1/accounts/search`, `/v1/contacts`, `/v1/contacts/search`, `/v1/opportunities`, and `/v1/opportunities/search`. The map key matches the key used under each record's `custom_fields` object. If the request uses `fields`, only selected custom fields are included; otherwise all visible custom fields for the object are included. For opportunities, definitions are scoped to the requested `pipeline_id`.

next_cursor.description: Cursor to pass on the next request when `has_more` is true.

limit.description: Effective page size used for this response.

AccountRecord.description: Account record. Additional selected system fields may be present.

ContactRecord.description: Contact record. Additional selected system fields may be present.

OpportunityRecord.description: Opportunity record. Additional selected system fields may be present.

BulkUpdateInput.description: Bulk update payload keyed by object UUID. For opportunities, relationship updates are expressed with system field names: `opportunity_account` for the associated account UUID, `opportunity_contact` for the primary contact UUID, and `opportunity_other_stakeholders` for the complete replacement list of additional contact UUIDs. These relationship fields replace the existing mapping for that field instead of appending.

additionalProperties.description: Patch object. For opportunity relationship fields, pass UUID strings for `opportunity_account` and `opportunity_contact`, and an array of contact UUID strings for `opportunity_other_stakeholders`.

field.description: Public field name, system field alias, or custom field slug.

operator.description: Filter operator. For multi-value operators such as `in`, `not_in`, `between`, `contains_all`, and `contains_none`, pass an array in `value`.

value.description: Filter value. For date/time fields such as `created_at`, `updated_at`, `close_date`, and custom date/timestamp fields, pass an ISO 8601 date or date-time string such as `2024-06-11T01:40:00Z`; Unix timestamps such as `1718070000` are not accepted in search filters. Use an array here for multi-value operators; omit for `empty` and `not_empty`.
